title: hieracium subramosum subsp. phaliotrichum
intro: Hieracium subramosum subsp. phaliotrichum, also known as Hawk's Beard, is a perennial plant species belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating gastrointestinal issues, and alleviating rheumatic pain. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
